g.data-narration: Welcome to Feature Engineering and Dimensionality Reduction - essential techniques for building effective machine learning models.
The curse of dimensionality occurs when too many features cause data to become sparse, making ML models struggle to find patterns.
g.text: 🔔 WHY THIS MATTERS: The Curse of Dimensionality
As features increase, data becomes sparse in high-dimensional space.
Distance metrics lose meaning → poor model performance.

g.data-narration: Feature engineering transforms raw data, while dimensionality reduction compresses information into fewer meaningful features.
Data cleaning handles missing values, outliers, and duplicates to ensure data quality before model training begins.
Feature selection chooses the most relevant features using correlation analysis, mutual information, and recursive feature elimination.
g.text: Mutual Information: I(X;Y) = H(X) + H(Y) - H(X,Y)
RFE: Recursively eliminate least important features

g.data-narration: Feature scaling normalizes feature magnitudes using min-max scaling or standard scaling for better model performance.
Principal Component Analysis finds orthogonal directions of maximum variance and projects data onto these principal components.
g.text: Finds directions of maximum variance in data
Formula: X_new = X × V (projection onto eigenvectors)

g.data-narration: Linear Discriminant Analysis finds projections that maximize class separability while minimizing within-class variance.
g.text: Supervised: Uses class labels to find optimal projection
Goal: Maximize between-class / within-class variance ratio

g.data-narration: t-SNE is a non-linear technique that preserves local structure, perfect for visualizing high-dimensional data in 2D or 3D.
Autoencoders are neural networks that learn to compress data into a bottleneck layer, capturing essential features non-linearly.
g.text: Neural network: Encoder → Latent space → Decoder

g.data-narration: Each technique has strengths: PCA is fast, LDA is supervised, t-SNE visualizes well, autoencoders handle complex patterns.
Feature engineering prepares data through cleaning, selection, and scaling. Dimensionality reduction uses PCA, LDA, t-SNE, or autoencoders to compress data effectively.
g.text: Master these techniques to build better ML models! 🚀
